title: [Feature]: Add a local Docker image and NATS Compose test stack
problem: Users can install the package with Python tooling and run systemd services, but local container-based testing still needs a maintained image build and a repeatable NATS JetStream deployment that exercises nats-sink without requiring Oracle credentials or local Python virtualenv setup.
proposal: Add a baseline Docker image for the nats-sink CLI and a JSON Compose stack that runs NATS JetStream, initializes a disposable stream, runs nats-sink with a mounted file-sink configuration, and publishes local test messages. Keep the image suitable for local testing and explicitly move production hardening and public image publishing into follow-up issues.
users: Maintainers, contributors, and operators who need a local container smoke test for nats-sinks with NATS JetStream and a disposable file sink destination.
delivery_semantics: Containerization must not change commit-then-ACK behavior. Shutdown handling must preserve the existing graceful stop model, and container health checks must reflect readiness without causing message loss.
security: The baseline local image must not contain credentials, local wallets, certificate material, production configs, or build secrets. It should avoid running as root where practical, document mounted configuration and output paths, and make clear that additional production hardening is tracked separately.
acceptance: A Dockerfile and .dockerignore are added for the nats-sink CLI image.
The image can run nats-sink with mounted JSON configuration.
A JSON Compose stack starts NATS JetStream and nats-sink for local file-sink testing.
A local smoke script can build the image, start the stack, publish messages, and verify output files when Docker is available.
Documentation explains the local Docker workflow, security boundaries, and limitations.
Follow-up issues exist for hardened production image work and release-driven public image publishing.
tests: Add deterministic unit tests for Docker and Compose assets. When Docker is available, run the local Docker smoke script. Run scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update docs/operations.md, docs/development.md, docs/security.md, README.md, docs/test-report.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after the baseline local Docker image and Compose test stack have merged into the release branch, with follow-up hardening and publishing issues created.
